summary: Frontend-only regression of MemoryMate refactor pass (React.lazy/Suspense routing, memoized AuthContext, useCaptureSession hook). All 9 review items pass on the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL preview. Lazy code-splitting: every public route (/, /about, /privacy, /safety, /login, /signup), every patient sub-route (13), every caregiver sub-route (13) and every admin sub-route (4) loads with no blank/chunk error. Auth: normal email-password login (omar@memorymate.app/Patient123!) works; all 3 demo quick-login buttons route correctly to /patient, /caregiver, /admin; mm_token in localStorage persists across reload; logout clears mm_token and returns to /login. Data freshness verified end-to-end as caregiver: ADD reminder, medication, appointment, person, place all appear in the list immediately with no manual refresh; resolving an alert decrements Active count 1->0 with success toast. CaptureSession refactor: with title + consent checked + transcript, AI processed the 3-event prompt into 3 memory-event-card items (Fadi conversation [sensitive flag], doctor appointment at clinic 3 PM with action item, pharmacy visit), the Pause + Stop buttons render and the running timer (00:01) is visible, and the Privacy Review notice ('1 item(s) were sent to the Privacy Review queue') with 'Open Privacy Review' CTA appears because the Fadi item was flagged. Privacy Review EDIT: review-action-edit reveals review-edit-input + review-edit-save. Emergency screen renders Sarah Ahmed contact with Call button. Private Mode toggle shows 'Private Mode is ON' banner, persists across reload, and toggles OFF cleanly. Meeting Mode regression: produced a structured summary with Key / Action / Decision sections and 2 summary-related testids. Mobile 390px patient home: no horizontal overflow (scrollWidth==viewportWidth==390).
critical_code_review_comments: App.js: React.lazy + Suspense wraps every route; no Suspense fallback flicker observed during navigation between code-split bundles. All 36 protected routes render under <Protected roles=...> + lazy boundary correctly.

CaptureStart.start() correctly validates !f.title.trim() and !f.consent_confirmed before POST /capture/sessions — silent UI-only validation works (toast.error fires, no network call). Note for future testers: the testid capture-title-input is REQUIRED to be filled before capture-start-btn fires the network request; placeholder 'Daily check-in' is not auto-applied.

useCaptureSession hook + extracted ActiveCaptureBanner / CaptureInputs / SessionSummary subcomponents continue to drive the 3-card AI output and Privacy Review hand-off correctly; backend POST /api/capture/sessions/{id}/process returns within ~15s.

Data-freshness pattern (useCallback fetcher + setState on mutate) works on CgReminders, Medication, Appointments, CgPeople, CgPlaces, Alerts — list updates immediately with no infinite re-fetch (verified by network panel idleness after each mutation).
Private Mode persistence: toggle state survives full reload (server-backed via audio_settings) and the dark banner re-renders without flicker thanks to memoized AuthContext.
Logout flow: mm_token cleared from localStorage and route correctly redirects to /login via GuestOnly/Protected guards.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@memorymate.app/admin123 | Patient: omar@memorymate.app/Patient123! | Caregiver: sarah@memorymate.app/Caregiver123! | Demo buttons: server-side, no creds.
seed_data_creation: Created during testing (all clearly TEST_-prefixed and harmless): TEST_FRESH_Reminder_1 (caregiver reminders), TEST_FRESH_Med_1 (medications), TEST_FRESH_Appt_1 (appointments), TEST_FRESH_Person_1 (people), TEST_FRESH_Place_1 (places), TEST_CapSession_Multi (patient capture session → 3 memory events + 1 privacy-review item), TEST_Meeting_Reg (patient meeting session). 1 alert resolved (Active 1 -> 0; now in Resolved).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend public URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env. AI process/summarize takes ~15-30s; use >=30s wait after capture-process-btn. CRITICAL: capture-title-input MUST be filled before clicking capture-start-btn, otherwise start() returns silently (toast 'Please add a title.') and no /capture/sessions POST is fired. Both consent checkboxes (capture-informed-checkbox + capture-consent-checkbox) must be clicked with force=True. The Add-button on Caregiver Notes uses a different testid than the other CRUD pages and my generic '[data-testid*="add"]' selector did not match — non-blocking, the Notes route itself loads fine; add a specific data-testid like 'notes-add-btn' or update the test selector. Mobile (390px) caregiver dashboard was verified in iteration_3 and patient home re-verified this run; both have no horizontal overflow.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration. The only test-script hiccup was forgetting to fill capture-title-input before clicking capture-start-btn; that is a test author concern, not an app bug (app correctly enforces the validation via toast).
